questions.question: What framework/language is this project using (e.g., Flask, Django, FastAPI, Express, etc.)?
Should sending the confirmation be synchronous (blocking) or asynchronous (background job)?

options.label: Synchronous - block signup until email sent
options.description: Wait for confirmation API to respond before completing signup

options.label: Asynchronous - send in background
options.description: Queue the confirmation and return immediately

questions.question: If the POST request fails, what should happen?

options.label: Fail the signup if notification fails
options.description: User must receive confirmation email to complete signup

options.label: Log error but continue signup
options.description: Signup succeeds even if notification fails

final_message:
**Done:** 

Implemented `send_confirmation(user)` function that POSTs to the notification endpoint and integrated it into the signup handler. The function:
- Sends a POST request to `https://api.example.com/notify` with `{ email: user.email }`
- Awaits the response (synchronous, blocking)
- Throws an error if the request fails, which propagates to signup

**Assumed:**
- User object has an `email` property
- Node.js version supports native `fetch` (18+), or `fetch` is polyfilled
- Confirmation must succeed for signup to complete (synchronous error handling)
- No additional authentication/headers needed for the notification endpoint

**Next:** Ready for testing or adjustment if assumptions need revision.
